theme: Welding a frame that flinches at every joint
title: Welding a Frame That Flinches at Every Joint
strategies: trivial: weld struts in input order, every side +1 (this is also the checker's own baseline construction)

greedy: weld struts in input order; choose each side to balance a RUNNING UNDISCOUNTED ledger per joint (the 'equal and opposite ratings cancel' intuition) -- never looks at the actual 1/(1+stiffness) divisor, so on components with several same-rating struts it leaves a large, systematic residual

strong: split the frame into its connected components, then run a seeded local search per component that simulates the EXACT stiffness-discounted accumulation while searching over weld order and side jointly -- exploits matched-stiffness pairing rather than matched-rating pairing
invalid: emits a weld-order list that omits/repeats a strut index
